Peter,

name: Cory Booker
subject: You around?
body:
| | |
---
| Friend -- I know, it's late. (And I definitely could use a good night's sleep.) But part of me is feeling hopeful right now, so I wanted to send a quick note. Here's why: When we wake up tomorrow, we'll be entering a new phase of this campaign. After seven months of building this operation, the pace will pick up -- fast. That's something to be excited about, and I know this team will be right there with me. Every day, we're seeing signs that we're starting to hit our stride. We got a poll this week that showed our support doubled in a matter of weeks. And we now know that I'll be one of only ten candidates who will be on stage at the September debate. **I 'm sending this note because it's all thanks to you.** You helped get me here. And I know I can count on you as this race grows intense this fall. Our monthly fundraising deadline is in just a few hours, and we're just a hair or two short of our goal.** **[It'd mean the world to get a donation from you to help reach it before the deadline.](https://act.corybooker.?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cory2020-em-august?refcode=em_fr_20190831_CB_20190831-fr-CB-eomfinal-actives_actives_l1&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=")** You're the best. I mean it.
Cory [](https://act.corybooker.?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cory2020-em-august?refcode=em_fr_20190831_CB_20190831-fr-CB-eomfinal-actives_actives_contribbutton&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=") **Trust me. I know what it's like to be short on hair. (OK, that dad joke is the sign that I really do need to get some sleep. Goodnight!)
---

---

This campaign is moving quickly. If you want the most up-to-date information, follow Cory on [Instagram](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc0b5a/5fca0d19/72d11645/2919b690/846977681/VEsC/?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://www.instagram.com/corybooker/"), [Twitter](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc0b5a/5fca0d19/72d11645/2919b69f/846977681/VEsD/?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://twitter.com/corybooker"), and [Facebook](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc0b5a/5fca0d19/72d11645/2919b69e/846977681/VEsA/?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://www.facebook.com/corybooker").
It's time for leadership that can reignite our spirit of common purpose. That takes all of us. We believe these emails are an important way to stay in touch, but if at any point you'd like to stop getting these updates, you can unsubscribe [here](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc0b5a/5fca0d19/72d11645/2919b69d/846977681/VEsB/?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://act.corybooker.com/unsubscribe").
Is this not your preferred email address? You can update your contact info [here](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc0b5a/5fca0d19/72d11645/2919b691/846977681/VEsO/?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://corybooker.com/join?source=em_emfooter").

name: Brandon Beach
subject: Midnight tonight
body:
Peter,

I know you are busy, so I will keep this brief.

If we want to change Washington, we need to start sending different types of leaders to Congress. We can't afford to elect radical liberals who do Nancy Pelosi's bidding. And we certainly cannot afford to elect Republicans who are more concerned with a title than working towards bold solutions.

That’s why I need your help now more than ever.

Please join our team by making an online donation before midnight tonight here: http://www.beachforcongress.com/#Donate

We are just $729 short of our monthly fundraising goal and we need all hands on deck now.

Make your donation here: http://www.beachforcongress.com/#Donate

Together will your help, we will #TakeBackTheSixth in 2020.

Brandon

name: Joe Sestak
subject: "More experience" on foreign policy than the rest of the field - my '4 the Record' Iowa TV interview
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Joe Sestak for President
body:
Peter,
I was just interviewed for** '4 the Record' with Jim Niedelman on Local 4 TV News (WHBF) in the Quad Cities - talking about Russia, Afghanistan, Syria and more (click below to watch).**
**[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/9563177/94619189/2096230920?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS83NTY5NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlZjU3YTk5OC1kY2NjLWU5MTEtYmNkMC0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=EfOGnT5QSp5hagelyv-ZgAZGLP21WYPdU42syV1D7vk=)**
Niedelman told viewers: “He arguably has **more experience** in this area than most of the rest of the field because of his military experience and the time he served on the National Security Council during the Clinton administration.”
I spoke of the challenges we face today, saying: “**The world has changed** ... it’s about using all the instruments of our power, bringing our allies together... so all follow the rules of the road on trade and diplomacy.”
**[Contribute](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/9563178/94619190/1036341175?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS83NTY5NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlZjU3YTk5OC1kY2NjLWU5MTEtYmNkMC0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=EfOGnT5QSp5hagelyv-ZgAZGLP21WYPdU42syV1D7vk=&refcode=em09012019niedelman)**
On Russia and China, I called for a new approach: “**There are sanctions upon Russia, and now Mr. Trump is thinking of removing them. Absolutely no way. They should be increased** \- also, as you remember recently, they commandeered Ukrainian warships and we did nothing. So we should be able to increase those sanctions... **Russia is basically a gas station.** Sanctions should have been applied right there, not only on their oligarchs, but on their ability to sell gas. Russia’s a problem. But it’s mainly a problem for their 'near abroad' countries such as Ukraine and Estonia... **the real global challenge is China**.”
I also shared thoughts on Afghanistan and Iraq: “We took forces and resources from Afghanistan, where we were succeeding in taking out the Taliban, and put them in Iraq. Washington can’t do two wars at once.”
**[Contribute](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/9563179/94619191/1036341175?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS83NTY5NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlZjU3YTk5OC1kY2NjLWU5MTEtYmNkMC0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=EfOGnT5QSp5hagelyv-ZgAZGLP21WYPdU42syV1D7vk=&refcode=em09012019niedelman)**
Asked what differentiated me from the current frontrunner, Joe Biden, I replied: “**I’m the Joe -- with all due respect to the Vice-President -- who, on Anita Hill, did stand up to Senator Arlen Specter, who tried to humiliate her... Joe Biden made a different decision. **Also there was **his** **vote for that tragic misadventure in Iraq** ... **we do need someone in the White House named Joe, but a Joe who understands the world and knows that before you use our military you need to know how it will end.**”
You can watch the foreign policy portion of my interview [here](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/9563180/94619192/2096230920?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS83NTY5NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlZjU3YTk5OC1kY2NjLWU5MTEtYmNkMC0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=EfOGnT5QSp5hagelyv-ZgAZGLP21WYPdU42syV1D7vk=). I also talked about education and training ([here](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/9563181/94619193/-154304713?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS83NTY5NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlZjU3YTk5OC1kY2NjLWU5MTEtYmNkMC0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=EfOGnT5QSp5hagelyv-ZgAZGLP21WYPdU42syV1D7vk=)) and reducing the power of corporations ([here](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/9563182/94619194/-239689753?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS83NTY5NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlZjU3YTk5OC1kY2NjLWU5MTEtYmNkMC0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=EfOGnT5QSp5hagelyv-ZgAZGLP21WYPdU42syV1D7vk=)). **If you agree that I am the candidate most uniquely qualified to be President and Commander-in-Chief, and to unite our country while convening the world for our collective good, please donate.**
Respectfully,
Joe Sestak
[Contribute](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/9563183/94619195/1036341175?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS83NTY5NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlZjU3YTk5OC1kY2NjLWU5MTEtYmNkMC0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=EfOGnT5QSp5hagelyv-ZgAZGLP21WYPdU42syV1D7vk=&refcode=em09012019niedelman)
Come on board. We encourage you to [visit our website](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/9563184/94619196/-209557350?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS83NTY5NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlZjU3YTk5OC1kY2NjLWU5MTEtYmNkMC0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=EfOGnT5QSp5hagelyv-ZgAZGLP21WYPdU42syV1D7vk=)

Absolutely no way. They should be increased \- also, as you remember recently, they commandeered Ukrainian warships and we did nothing. So we should be able to increase those sanctions... Russia is basically a gas station. Sanctions should have been applied right there, not only on their oligarchs, but on their ability to sell gas. Russia’s a problem. But it’s mainly a problem for their 'near abroad' countries such as Ukraine and Estonia... the real global challenge is China.” I also shared thoughts on Afghanistan and Iraq: “We took forces and resources from Afghanistan, where we were succeeding in taking out the Taliban, and put them in Iraq. name: Dan Baer
subject: Labor Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dan for Colorado
body:
I wanted to take a moment to share with you what I am thinking about this Labor Day Weekend
|
---
[ ](https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/m/5fb827c5/232dfd1/815199e/62a1cbd5/3365870164/VEsH/?g=JnF4Cq_HJ6FcyTX3h0hkK5A "https://www.danforcolorado.com")

| | Friend, I wanted to take a moment to share with you what I am thinking about this Labor Day weekend. Labor Day grew out of the Industrial Revolution. At the time, the United States experienced a dramatic increase in wealth and economic productivity. But the gains accrued primarily to those at the top, while the average American worked extremely long hours, seven days a week, for very low pay, and in dangerous working conditions to make ends meet. Workers responded by banding together in unions and fighting for structural change. They demanded standards we take for granted today, like rules around child labor, workplace safety, and an eight hour workday. We can draw a direct line to tomorrow's holiday from the largest and bloodiest of those strikes, the Pullman Strike in 1894, when 150,000 workers around the country joined together to demand better treatment. Congress recognized the efforts of the labor movement, and the sacrifice of those workers, by declaring a federal holiday called Labor Day. It is unacceptable that 100 years later, for so many, Labor Day does not mark a day to celebrate or rest. It's just another day when too many working families will struggle with anxiety over how to make ends meet. Structural problems in our politics and our economy have left 80% of Americans without a real wage increase in decades, and leave them without the means to afford the basic elements of a middle class life - a home, an education, and healthcare. One in six children--including many children of people who work--is likely to go to bed hungry tonight in this country. This Labor Day weekend, instead of just thanking working Americans and paying tribute to the labor movement, let's do them justice. Let's do them justice by pursuing the structural changes needed to fix a broken system that produces tax breaks for billionaires but lets workers' salaries stagnate and the budget deficit explode, all while making it harder to organize to demand change. Let's do them justice by fixing a healthcare system where people are dying or going bankrupt because of health care costs while drug companies are allowed to price gouge on products developed through taxpayer-funded research. Let's do them justice by reforming an education system where educators are stuck with tens of thousands of dollars in student loans, and get paid less than a living wage in the communities they serve. This can be done. It won't be easy, and sometimes people tell me that the odds of getting the change we want are slim. But I will stay in the fight, and I believe that together, we can mend our country and win the future. We can build an economy that rewards innovation not exploitation, and that has real, fair competition that drives improved standards of living for the vast majority of Americans, rather than one that rests on rent-seeking and monopolistic power and contributes to widening inequality. I hope you will join me in speaking to the structural problems our country faces and energetically advocate for the bold, progressive steps needed to fix them. In solidarity, Dan |
---
[](https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/m/5fb827c5/232dfd1/815199e/62a1cbda/3365870164/VEsE/?g=JnF4Cq_HJ6FcyTX3h0hkK5A "https://www.facebook.com/DanForColorado/") [](https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/m/5fb827c5/232dfd1/815199e/62a1cbdb/3365870164/VEsF/?g=JnF4Cq_HJ6FcyTX3h0hkK5A "https://twitter.com/danbbaer")

Colorado is the most important Senate race in the entire country according to Nate Silver and CNN. It's our only chance of flipping the Senate. And to win the most important Senate race, we need the most qualified candidate.

Dan served as an Ambassador under President Obama and as the head of the Colorado Department of Higher Education in Governor Hickenlooper's cabinet. If elected, he would be the first openly gay man to serve in Senate history.

We know we send you a lot of emails, but it's because we're running a grassroots campaign and rejecting corporate PAC money. We need your help to win -- and grassroots donations like yours truly make the difference. | | [**DONATE**](https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/m/5fb827c5/232dfd1/815199e/62a1cbd8/3365870164/VEsC/?g=JnF4Cq_HJ6FcyTX3h0hkK5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/baer-email?refcode=footer&amount=25&recurring=on&recur_weekly=on")

We hope you'll consider sticking with us long-term. name: Cory Booker
subject: Will you join me in New York City for the climate town hall?
body:
| | |
---
| The climate town hall is right around the corner. And team, I want you to be my guest. I'm deeply grateful for everyone who's chipped in and helped power this campaign.

Side by side, we're going to keep fighting for an America that works for everyone, not just a thinner and thinner slice at the top. And we're building our grassroots movement without taking any money from private fundraising events, Washington lobbyists, or PACs.

People like you make this movement possible. Tomorrow, I'll be back in New Hampshire for a house party to talk about the big, structural change we need to see. We'll dream big, fight hard, and win.

Elizabeth

name: Beto O'Rourke
subject: Midland and Odessa
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY BETO FOR AMERICA
body:
I called Amy last night after I’d learned about the murders in Midland and
Odessa.

I said, “I don’t feel right.”

I know this happens all the time. In fact, this is the fourth massacre in
Texas in under two years. And just last year alone there were 340 mass
shootings in America. Amy and I were just talking about this same thing
the night before because we’d both read news stories about a shooting at a
high school football game in Mobile, Alabama.

In other words, this shouldn’t have come as a surprise. Because of our
country’s deference to the gun lobby and the NRA at the expense of the
lives of our fellow Americans — our unwillingness to pass background
checks, red flag laws; our inability to stop selling weapons of war and
our failure to buy those back that have already been sold... because of
what we’ve accepted, our complicity in 100 gun deaths a day... because of
all of this, logically, this was bound to happen.
So what made me feel so sick, so sad, so angry?

Maybe it was because it hadn’t even been a month since the last mass
shooting in West Texas, when 22 people were killed at a Walmart in El
Paso.

Maybe in the aftermath we all took some false comfort, like lightning
couldn’t strike twice.

Because when it comes down to it, we look at these shootings like we would
a lightning strike, a natural disaster, an act of God, something you
couldn’t predict or plan for, couldn’t stop even if you wanted to. Just
gotta take your chances and accept that it happens.

Like lightning, we think about how to protect ourselves despite the threat
— bulletproof backpacks for our kids; active shooter drills in our
schools; deciding what window you’re going to jump out of, which bookshelf
you’ll pull down, how you’ll get your kid to safety or how you might have
to shelter them by sacrificing your body and your life.

Now, *you* may not look at it that way... You may be the one organizing
the march for our lives, the walkout from your school, the local Moms
Demand chapter. But, *we*, we are all in this together, for good or for
bad. For life or for death. And in a government of, by and for the people,
we judge ourselves by whether we’ve changed this, or failed to change
this.

And so far, we’ve absolutely failed to change this.

On that phone call last night, Amy said, “I don’t feel right, either.”

We feel sick for the families in Midland and Odessa. The parents who lost
a child. The parents who are with their child in the intensive care unit
in Lubbock. The families who are in shock and in mourning. The many
friends we have there, who we called, who assured us they were okay — but
shaken up. Just left the lockdown at the Target, or the Young Democrats
meeting at the hotel, or wherever they were when the first reports of an
active shooter came in.

We talked about how there is so much hatred and pain in this country right
now. Anger and racism and a divide between us that seems to grow every
day. Paralyzing. The sense of common purpose, an ability to focus on what
we have to do together to save ourselves and one another, lost. How do we
find our way back? I told her about a story I’d heard of London during the
Nazi blitz (German for “lightning”) in 1940 and 1941, how in the face of
indiscriminate terror and death a city and a nation united in common
purpose and common cause. Londoners stopped focusing on their petty
differences, their personal disappointments, their private despair — and
did everything they could to save the lives of their fellow humans and to
unite in confronting the Nazi menace.

How do we approach this menace of gun violence that kills 40,000 of us a
year in the same way?

I read something Rabbi Michael Latz wrote this morning. He was responding
to criticism that using profanity to describe this horror — the death toll
in Midland and Odessa now stands at 7, including a 15-year old girl who
attended Odessa high school, with 19 injured, among them a 17-month old
child — and our complicity in it was wrong. To those concerned about
calling a fucked up situation “fucked up,” he said:

“Profanity ain't the F-bomb. Profanity is living in a country where a 17
MONTH OLD BABY IS SHOT IN THE FACE...”

He reminds us that we must see what is happening clearly, speak about it
honestly and act decisively.

I’ve listened to you as I’ve traveled the country and have written your
experiences, your ideas and your leadership into our plan to stop gun
violence, [ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/31096?t=1001&akid=52542%2E444211%2E6x_Ha2 ]which you can read here.

But we can’t wait that long to act. Your voice is needed right now. So
here’s what we must do now.

1. When a victims fund is stood up -- donate. I’ll be sure to send a
reminder. The family of the 17 month old, Anderson Davis, [ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/31097?t=1002&akid=52542%2E444211%2E6x_Ha2 ]has a
GoFundMe. We know from our experience in El Paso that families will
need help with medical bills, the cost of funerals, making up for lost
income, and paying the rent. Do this.

2. Call your federal representative in Congress and your U.S. Senator at
(202) 224-3121. Let them know you want them fighting with all they’ve
got for universal background checks, for red flag laws and an end to
the sale of weapons of war. And if you want them to take the next,
necessary step, tell them you support mandatory licensing and a
mandatory buyback of assault weapons. If they don’t hear you and fail
to take action, you will replace them in this next election (in
Virginia, that’s in November of THIS YEAR).

3. If you’re in Texas, [ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/31098?t=1003&akid=52542%2E444211%2E6x_Ha2 ]call your state rep and state senator and let
them know that the 10 laws that just went into effect today that
LOOSEN instead of TIGHTEN restrictions on guns need to be repealed. No
reason we should make it easier for people to bring guns near or into
schools, churches, mosques, synagogues and foster care homes. No
reason we should prevent landlords and private property owners from
restricting the use of guns on their property.

4. [ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/31099?t=1004&akid=52542%2E444211%2E6x_Ha2 ]Make sure you are registered to vote. Make sure your mom and little
brother, your classmates, your colleagues, your neighbors, your
friends on Facebook are all registered to vote. And then make sure
each and every person in your life votes in every election going
forward until we have leaders who represent our values and our lives.

5. Join a [ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/31102?t=1005&akid=52542%2E444211%2E6x_Ha2 ]Moms Demand or [ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/31101?t=1006&akid=52542%2E444211%2E6x_Ha2 ]March for Our Lives chapter in your state.

6. Don’t give up or give in. Keep up the faith and the pressure. Let’s
change this now.

- Beto

Contribute to Beto's campaign: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/beto-for-america-emails?refcode=em20190901footer&t=1007&refcode2=52542_444211_6x_Ha2&akid=52542%2E444211%2E6x_Ha2

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to act. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://act.betoorourke.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1008&akid=52542%2E444211%2E6x_Ha2

Join a [ ]Moms Demand or [ ]March for Our Lives chapter in your state. 6. Don’t give up or give in. Keep up the faith and the pressure. Let’s change this now. - Beto Contribute to Beto's campaign:
